attributes.description:
Defines the current visibility and icon of the burger-menu icon.
The menu button is added automatically if the component finds a syn-side-nav in
variant="default".
The following values can be used:
- hidden: The burger menu is not visible
- open: The burger menu is visible and shows the close icon
- closed: The burger menu is visible and shows the open icon
attributes.name: burger-menu

attributes.description: The headers label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.
attributes.name: label

attributes.description:
Makes the header stick to the top of the viewport when scrolling.
Also applies a shadow to the header when scrolling.
attributes.name: sticky

cssParts.description: The button that toggles the burger menu
cssParts.name: burger-menu-toggle-button

cssParts.description: The wrapper most content items reside
cssParts.name: content

cssParts.description: The element wrapping the application name
cssParts.name: label

cssParts.description: The wrapper the application logo resides in
cssParts.name: logo

cssParts.description: The Item wrapping the optional application menu
cssParts.name: meta-navigation

cssParts.description: The wrapper that is holding the optional top navigation section
cssParts.name: navigation

events.description: Emitted when the burger menu is toggled to closed
events.name: syn-burger-menu-closed

events.description: Emitted when the burger menu is toggled to hidden
events.name: syn-burger-menu-hidden

events.description: Emitted when the burger menu is toggled to open
events.name: syn-burger-menu-open

methods.description:
Connect a `syn-side-nav` to add automatic interaction of the header with the side navigation
like showing the burger menu icon and open / close handling.

If no side navigation is connected, the header will use the first `syn-side-nav` element it
finds.
methods.name: connectSideNavigation

properties.description:
Defines the current visibility and icon of the burger-menu icon.
The menu button is added automatically if the component finds a syn-side-nav in
variant="default".
The following values can be used:
- hidden: The burger menu is not visible
- open: The burger menu is visible and shows the close icon
- closed: The burger menu is visible and shows the open icon
properties.name: burgerMenu

properties.description: The headers label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.
properties.name: label

properties.description:
Makes the header stick to the top of the viewport when scrolling.
Also applies a shadow to the header when scrolling.
properties.name: sticky

slots.description: The logo that should be displayed. Will fall back to the SICK logo if not provided
slots.name: logo

slots.description: The meta-navigation is used to add various application toolbar icons Best used with `<syn-icon-button />` and `<syn-drop-down />`
slots.name: meta-navigation

slots.description: This slot can be used to add an optional horizontal navigation
slots.name: navigation

slots.description: An icon to use in lieu of the default burger-menu=open state. The default close icon is a 'x'.
slots.name: open-burger-menu-icon

slots.description: An icon to use in lieu of the default burger-menu=closed state. The default open icon is a burger menu.
slots.name: closed-burger-menu-icon

summary:
The <syn-header /> element provides a generic application header
that can be used to add applications name, toolbar and primary navigation.
